# UC-11.3.64 · Proofpoint Email Gateway TLS and Transport Posture (MTA Logs)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Compliance &middot; **Status:** Draft

*We watch how Proofpoint treats and rewrites mail, so threats and DLP events line up with what the security team already sees in their console.*

---

## Description

Inbound or outbound messages where SMTP TLS was not used exposes messages to passive surveillance and fails many contractual encryption expectations.

## Value

Extends the same TLS visibility pattern used in HIPAA-style audits to continuous monitoring, letting messaging teams chase down legacy apps still sending cleartext.

## Implementation

Field names (`smtp_tls_status`, `rcpt`, `client_ip`) depend on your Proofpoint log export and Splunk parser—align with `props.conf` from your TA. Scope to regulated business units via recipient domain lookups. Pair with connector remediation tickets.

## SPL

```spl
index=mail sourcetype="proofpoint:mta" earliest=-24h
| eval tls=lower(coalesce(smtp_tls_status, TLS_Status, tls_status, ""))
| eval tls_ok=if(match(tls,"none|no|plaintext|not used|off"),0,1)
| where tls_ok=0
| stats count values(rcpt) as sample_rcpt by sender, client_ip
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (sender IP × recipient samples), map of client_ip, timeline of cleartext events.

## Known False Positives

Opportunistic TLS retries, old appliances, and partner MTAs on legacy ciphers in transition.

## References

- [Proofpoint Email Security integrations](https://www.proofpoint.com/us/products/email-security)
- [Proofpoint On Demand Email Security Add-on (Splunkbase)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4327)
